text:
Once upon a time in a quaint little village nestled between rolling hills, there lived a humble baker named Bertha. Bertha’s bakery was famous—not just for its delicious bread—but for the enchanted breadths that animals would unexpectedly visit. There was Mr. Mouse who loved big chunks of cheese, Mrs. Hedgehog with a sweet tooth for honey doughnuts, and the clever owl who just couldn't resist a warm slice fresh out of the oven.

One particularly bright morning, as Bertha was kneading rolls that seemed to sing with their own tiny melody, a mysterious fog rolled in. It wasn’t just any fog; it shimmered with an otherworldly glow. Birds ceased their song mid-flight.

From the mist emerged a wise old owl, perched serenely on a branch. “Ah,” he declared softly, “the stars are brighter today, the sun laughs warmer. Something strange is afoot.”

Bertha looked up. “What strange thing?”

“The breadthroths,”
